per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain usually starts around 2-3 times a day, it's this crampy feeling that gets worse and worse until I have to go to the bathroom. When I do, it feels like my bowel is just not emptying properly, which can be really frustrating.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Besides the abdominal pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing some joint pain - achy feeling in both knees and ankles, but no swelling or redness.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I actually have had a low-grade fever a few times a week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past 3 weeks, and my appetite has been affected too.

I've actually been drinking more than usual lately - I'd say 2-3 beers most weeknights, but I'm trying to cut back due to stress from work and other things going on in my life. And no, I'm not a smoker.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My sister's been dealing with a serious condition for years and has had to undergo some tough treatments. Other than that, I don't really have any close relatives who've had major health issues.
